design_concept: High-contrast neo-brutalism meets Swiss grid. Raw HTML feel with sharp borders. Color used purely as a function of status. Strong emphasis on data legibility and 'command-center' operations for Discord bots.

typography.pairing_strategy: The Swiss Brutalist: One expressive display head (Cabinet Grotesk), clean sans for body (IBM Plex Sans), and strict monospace (JetBrains Mono) for verification codes and data logs.

scale.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl tracking-tighter leading-none font-black
scale.h2: text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl tracking-tight leading-tight font-bold

spacing_and_layout.strategy: Strict grid hierarchy, dense for dashboards, wide for marketing. Generous padding in containers.
spacing_and_layout.alignment: Optical alignment. Left-aligned text heavily enforced for readability. Headings left-aligned.

surfaces_and_borders.card: border-2 border-[#09090B] bg-white rounded-none shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_#09090B] hover:shadow-[2px_2px_0px_0px_#09090B] hover:translate-x-[2px] hover:translate-y-[2px] transition-all duration-100
surfaces_and_borders.input: border-2 border-[#09090B] bg-white rounded-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#002FA7] focus:border-[#002FA7] focus:outline-none p-3

buttons.primary: border-2 border-[#09090B] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest bg-[#09090B] text-white hover:bg-[#002FA7] hover:text-white transition-colors rounded-none px-6 py-3 shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,0.2)] active:shadow-none active:translate-x-[4px] active:translate-y-[4px]
buttons.secondary: border-2 border-[#09090B] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest bg-white text-[#09090B] hover:bg-[#F4F4F5] transition-colors rounded-none px-6 py-3 shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_#09090B] active:shadow-none active:translate-x-[4px] active:translate-y-[4px]

status_badges.verified: font-mono text-xs uppercase px-2 py-1 border-2 border-[#10B981] bg-[#10B981]/10 text-[#10B981] inline-flex items-center gap-2
status_badges.pending: font-mono text-xs uppercase px-2 py-1 border-2 border-[#F59E0B] bg-[#F59E0B]/10 text-[#F59E0B] inline-flex items-center gap-2
status_badges.failed: font-mono text-xs uppercase px-2 py-1 border-2 border-[#EF4444] bg-[#EF4444]/10 text-[#EF4444] inline-flex items-center gap-2

components.tables: w-full text-left border-collapse border-2 border-[#09090B]. th and td should have border-b-2 border-r-2 border-[#09090B].
components.navigation: Sticky top nav with glassmorphism surface, 2px bottom border, high contrast logo.
components.code_block: font-mono bg-[#F4F4F5] border-2 border-[#09090B] p-4 text-sm relative group

animations.hover: Harsh, instant transitions or brutalist push-down effects (shadow disappears, element moves down). Duration 100ms.
animations.loading: Monospace ASCII blinking cursor `_` or raw text `LOADING...`.
animations.page_transition: None. Keep it raw and instant.

icons.style: Bold or Duotone, 24px default size, strict black or accent colors.

images.description: High-contrast Swiss style poster, black and white with one bold Klein Blue accent. Use this in the landing page hero section.
Abstract data visualization, black grids on white background. Good for empty states or dashboard intro panels.
Dynamic black and white diagonal stripes. Useful for decorative banners or footer backgrounds.

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes formatted in kebab-case (e.g., data-testid='verify-account-btn').
Use pure HTML + Tailwind for the marketing landing page, prioritizing grid asymmetry and the Swiss Brutalism look.

Use Shadcn components for the dashboard, but HEAVILY CUSTOMIZE them. Strip all rounded corners (use rounded-none), remove soft gray shadows, and apply solid 2px black borders with solid block drop shadows.
Use JetBrains Mono or a similar monospace font for the verification codes the user must paste in their bio.
Do not use generic gray gradients. Stick to stark white, black borders, and pure accent colors.
Implement brutalist hover states: transform translate to mimic pressing a physical button with solid black drop shadows.
For the 'how to use' slash commands guide, display them like terminal inputs in monospace blocks.
